title: rubus × aciphylloides
intro: Rubus × aciphylloides, also known as the thimbleberry, is a hybrid of the Pacific raspberry (Rubus spectabilis) and the black raspberry (Rubus occidentalis) and is native to North America. The thimbleberry is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of anthocyanins and ellagic acid, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus × aciphylloides include its potential to reduce the risk of certain cancers, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of the thimbleberry can be found in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and jams, and are often used to make medicines and other health-related products.
